titleStmt.title: Lamentation of the Soul (Saqoqāwa nafs), The Wisest among the Wise (Ṭabiba ṭabibān), Malkǝʾ-hymn to Walatta Ṗeṭros

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

altIdentifier.note: The manuscript was photographed by EMIP as 03459

msItem.incipit: ፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ዘነበበ። በነቢያት፡ እወጥን፡ አንሰ፡ ሰቆቃወ፡ ነፍስ፡ ዕፁበ። ኵሎ፡ ሌሊተ፡ በአንብዕ፡ እንዘ፡ አርሕስ፡ ምስካበ። ከመ፡ በአንብዑ፡ ዳዊት፡ ዓራቶ፡ ሐፀበ። ለነፍስ፡ ወናዛዚሃ፡ በሕቁ።
msItem.explicit: ተመየጢ፡ ለፍትወተ፡ ሥጋ፡ እምዕበዱ። መንገለ፡ ንስሐ፡ ነፍስየ፡ እምቅድመ፡ ትፃኢ፡ እምዓፀዱ። አመ፡ ዕለተ፡ ደይንሰ፡ እምኵነኔ፡ አምላክ፡ አሐዱ። ኢይትመየጥ፡ አብ፡ ለአድኅኖ፡ ኀበ፡ ነፍሰ፡ ኃጥእ፡ ወልዱ። እስመ፡ ኵሉ፡ ይትፈደይ፡ በዘገብረ፡ እዱ፡ አኃውየ፡ በእንተ፡ ጌጋይየ፡ እብኪ። ኀበ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ መላኪ። እስመ፡ መሐሪ፡ ውእቱ፡ ወመፍቀሬ፡ ሰብእ፡ መሐኪ።

msItem.incipit: በስመ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅ ዱስ፡ ዘነበበ። በነቢያት፡ ቅድመ፡ ወበሐዋርያት፡ ካዕበ። እዌጥን፡ አንሰ፡ ሰቆ ቃወ፡ ነፍስ፡ ዕፁበ። ኵሎ፡ ሌሊተ፡ በአንብዕ፡ እንዘ፡ አርሕስ፡ ምስካበ። ከመ፡ በአንብዑ፡ ዳዊት፡ ዓራቶ፡ ሐፀበ። ሐዋፂሃ፡ ለነፍስ፡ ወናዛዚሃ፡ በሕቁ። እምነ፡ ኀዘናት፡ አእላፍ፡ ዘኢይትኌለቁ።
msItem.explicit: ስብሐት፡ ለከ፡ አምላኪየ፡ ወበጸጋ፡ ጥምቀት፡ አቡየ። እመ፡ አድኀንከ፡ እምሲኦል፡ ለኃጥእት፡ ነፍስየ። በኍልቈ፡ ደቂቁ፡ ለያዕቆብ፡ እለ፡ ወረዱ፡ ምድረ፡ ግብጽ። ሰቆቃወ፡ ነፍስ፡ ስብኪ፡ በውስተ፡ ኵሉ፡ አንቀጽ። ወኢትፃኢ፡ ነፍስየ፡ እምቤተ፡ ንስሐ፡ ሕንጹ። ራትዕ፡ መልአከ፡ ሞት፡ ዘኢያደሉ፡ ለገጽ። እስመ፡ ይነሥአኪ፡ ግብተ፡ በዘመን፡ ሕፁፅ።

msItem.incipit: ኦእግዚአብሔር፡ ጠቢበ፡ ጠቢባን፡ ከሀሊ። እስከ፡ ለዓለም፡ እምጥንት፡ እንተ፡ ኢትበሊ። ድኩመ፡ ሕሊና፡ ለገብርከ፡ ወፈራሀ፡ ልብ፡ ኢተሐባሊ። እስመ፡ ኀቤከ፡ እግዚኦ፡ ጸሎተ፡ አድኅኖ፡ እጼሊ። አድኅነኒ፡ አድኅነኒ፡ እምኵሉ፡ ቀታሊ።
msItem.explicit:
ዘገደፍኮሙ፡ ለሰማዕታት፡ በብዝኃ፡ ሥቃያት፡ ይሕምሙ። እግዚአብሔር፡ ተባረክ፡ ለኂሩት፡ ማኅተሙ።
አኮ፡ በዝንቱ፡ በልሳነ፡ ሥጋ፡ ተናጋሪ። ወኢበዝ፡ ቃል፡ ሕንፃ፡ መሬት፡ ተመዝባሪ። አላ፡ በዝኩ፡ ቃለ፡ መንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ነባሪ። በዝ፡ ዓለም፡ አአኵተከ፡ ፈጣሪ። በዘይመጽእኒ፡ በዓለም፡ ደኃሪ።

msItem.incipit: በስመ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ዘሀሎ፡ ወአመ፡ ኢሀሎ፡ ዘመን። ወኢቀደሞ፡ ሰአት፡ መጠነ፡ ቅጽበታ፡ ለዓይን። እሰንቁ፡ ለኪ፡ አውታረ፡ ነባቢ፡ ድርሳን። ያበርህ፡ ውስተ፡ ልብየ፡ ወለተ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ምእመን፡ ፀዳለ፡ አእምሮ፡ ንስቲተ፡ ሲኖዳ፡ ካህን። ሰላም፡ ለዝክረ፡ ስምኪ፡ ዘቀጸላ፡ ሞገስ፡ ትርሲቱ። ወለሥዕርትኪ፡ ስውጥ፡ ዘቅብዓ፡ ቅዳሴ፡ ውስቴቱ፡
msItem.explicit: ወለተ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ አኮኑ፡ በእንተ፡ ዝንቱ፡ ነገር። ይትፌሣሕ፡ ሰማይ፡ ወትትሐሠይ፡ ምድር። ወይወውዑ፡ አድባር፡ ወአውግር። ሰላም፡ ለፍልሰተ፡ ሥጋኪ፡ እንተ፡ አርአየ፡ መንክረ። እምፍልሰተ፡ ጻድቃን፡ አዕላፍ፡ እለ፡ ከሠቱ፡ ምስጢረ። ወለተ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ሐመር፡ እንተ፡ ታነጽሒ፡ ነውረ። ሰውርኒ፡ ውስተ፡ ሕፅንኪ፡ ከመ፡ ኢይርአየኒ፡ ምዕረ። ዓይነ፡ መልአከ፡ ሞት፡ ጽኑዕ፡ ዘያየብስ፡ ባሕር።

supportDesc.foliation: Pencil foliation in the upper right corner (1–60).
supportDesc.condition: The bookblock has been damaged by humidity, especially in the lower outer margins, causing the deformation of the parchment support in correspondence of the outer corners and the formation of dark spots, possibly mould (e.g. ) Stains of various origins are present both on the leather cover and the bookblock (e.g. ). The sewing is broken and so are the headband tiedowns. is becoming detached from the bookblock. The leather cover is abraded in several points.

layout.note: Data on text area and margin dimensions taken from .
layout.ab: Ruling and pricking are visible. Both primary and ruling pricks are visible.
The upper and bottom lines are written above the ruling.

layout.note: Each page accommodates 3 or 4 written lines per column, starting from the top. The rest of the page invariably contains a miniature, each depicting a biblical scene. All miniatures reach the bottom of the page. Data on text area and margin dimensions taken from .
layout.ab: Ruling and pricking are visible. Both primary and ruling pricks are visible.
The upper and bottom lines are written above the ruling.

layout.note: Data on text area and margin dimensions taken from .
The characters per line are 12–15. The end of lines is very irregular.
layout.ab: Ruling and pricking are visible. Both primary and ruling pricks are visible.
The upper and bottom lines are written above the ruling.

handNote.date: First half of the eighteenth century.
handNote.desc: Elegant and uniform handwriting. Characters are slanted and regularly spaced.
handNote.ab: The explicit of the Lamentation of the Soul on terminates with a chain of red and black dots.

handNote.desc: Decent handwriting. Characters are executed with a thin nib and are uniformly spaced.
handNote.seg: Few lines on the incipit page, alternating with black lines; holy names, included that of Walatta Ṗeṭros; the word salām; elements of the punctuation signs, of the numerals, and of the text dividers.
handNote.ab: The text of the Malkǝʾ-hymn to Walatta Ṗeṭros accommodates a chain of red and black dots on .

decoDesc.summary:
Almost every page in this lavishly decorated manuscript features an illumination. In most of them, God the Father is shown intervening in episodes from the Old and New Testaments arranged in loose chronological order. The images found in this volume bear comparison with those found in another illustrated copy of the same text kept at the British Library (MS London, British Library, Or. 590). The two manuscripts were painted by different hands, but many of the images are almost identical in terms of layout and iconography, though there are a number of exceptions. For instance, the giants that appear on appear to be female in this copy and male in the British Library copy; and in this manuscript the scene of the Binding of Isaac () is less detailed than in the British Library example, where Abraham is shown riding a donkey. Likewise, the tent on is closed in this manuscript, but open in the one from the British Library. At times the images in the Bodleian Libraries manuscript have more details, whereas in other cases it is the opposite. In the Bodleian example, a donor is present beneath a representation of the Trinity, whereas in the British Library example there is no donor and God is represented alone. The number of images also varies: the British Library copy features representations of the Crucifixion and Descent into Hell that are not present in the Bodleian copy. Finally, the images in the latter manuscript do not have captions, whereas in this piece they are numerous and added next to the figures by the main hand that wrote titles in red ink on the upper margin of each folio. It is unclear if these rubricated lines of text in the upper margin of the folio refer to the illuminations, the text, or both, but they have been systematically transcribed here alongside those other captions that are clearly associated with the images. God the Father appears in almost all of the miniatures. As shown by
174–177 , several images in these manuscripts were inspired by European prints or paintings. Many other details evoke local customs and material traditions, such as the prayer stick on , the drum on , the warriors wearing leopard skins and headbands on , the king riding a caparisoned mule with bells on its bridle and accompanied by a page bearing an umbrella on , the butchering of animals on , the presence of what appears to be a mottled cow hide on a tent on , and the bread basket on . Particularly interesting is the representation of the miraculous sleep of Ebed-Melech (Abimelech) the Ethiopian on , which offers a reference to 4 Baruch that is only canonical in the Ethiopic tradition, since the episode is not mentioned in the text that the image accompanies and to Ethiopian Maccabees on . This scene is absent in the British Library copy. On , we see a reference to the translation of the Bible into Greek mentioned in the Ethiopian Synaxarion; the artist showcases his familiarity with the story and expands on the text of the Wisest of the Wise by including a reference to the angel that stops Simeon from changing the words of the Bible. In another instance, on , both text and image rely on non-biblical sources when they refer to Alexander the Great as presenting offerings to the High Priest of the Israelites. Alexander is shown presenting a chest, a detail possibly inspired by the Josippon (
409–410 ). Noteworthy is also the decision of the artists to represent King Nebuchadnezzar as a wild boar when he is transformed into a beast by God, a detail that is not mentioned in the Bible and recalls the transformation of Tiridates III of Armenia. Some motifs on the clothes of prominent figures in the illustrations may provide evidence of the circulation of particular patterns on textiles. The ones that appear on , for example, seem to be inspired by contemporary Indian woven silks, as a sign of their prestige, whereas the fabric that covers the altar behind them could have been inspired by a block-printed Gujarati textile. Relatedly, the attire of Goliath on may have been inspired by chainmail armour and a pointed helmet in use in the Islamicate world. Many scenes feature details inspired by the biblical texts that inspired the verses.

decoNote.desc: God the Father, identified by the caption እግዚአብሔር።. The donor Takla Māryām is mentioned in the main legend. እግዚአብሔር፡ አብ፡ አኀዜ፡ ኵሉ፡ ዓለም። አድኅኖ፡ ለገብርከ፡ ተክለ፡ ማርያም፡
God creates life and the elements of the world (fire, water, earth, air) as angels praise him. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ሰማይ።, እሳት።, ነፍስ።, መሬት።, and ማይ።. ዘከመ፡ ፈጠረ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ፍጥረታተ፡ በዕለተ፡ እኁድ፡ በአርምሞ፡ ወነቢብ።
God the Father, flanked by Michael and Gabriel, casts the devil out of heaven and binds him. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል።, and ዲያብሎስ።. ዘከመ፡ ወድቀ፡ ዲያብሎስ።
God the Father binds the angels in Hell. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ገጸ፡ ሰብእ፡, ገጸ፡ ንስር።, ገጸ፡ አንበሳ።, ገጸ፡ ላህም።, and አጋንንት. ዘከመ፡ ወድቁ፡ አጋንንት።
God the Father creates Adam in his likeness. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, አዳም፡, እሳት፡, ነፋስ፡, መሬት፡, and ማይ።. ዘከመ፡ ፈጠሮ፡ ለአዳም። እም፬ቱ፡ ጠባይዓት።
God the Father creates Eve from Adam’s rib in a garden with flowers. Captions: አዳም፡, ሔዋን፡, and እግዚአብሔር።. ዘከመ፡ ወፅአት፡ ሔዋን፡ እምገቦሁ፡ ለአዳም።

Two scenes: Eve and Adam share the forbidden fruit taken from a fig tree with the serpent coiled around it (left); the pair are cast out of Eden by an angel (right). Captions: ከይሲ፡, አዳም።, ሔዋን፤, and መልአክ፡. ዘከመ፡ አስሐቶሙ፡ ከይሲ፡ ለአዳም፡ ወለሔዋን።

God the Father looks down upon Adam and Eve, who are shown ashamed of their nakedness to the left and clothed to the right. Captions: ዘከመ፡ ተዓርቁ፡ እምልብስ፡ አዳም፡ ወሔዋን።, አዳም፡, and ሔዋን።. ዘከመ፡ ናዘዞሙ፡ ለአዳም፡ ወለሔዋን።

Three episodes from top left to bottom right: Cain and Abel offer gifts to God (above); Cain kills his brother (bottom left); he encounters a crow while wandering (bottom right). Captions: አቤል፡, መሥዋዕት፡, እሳት፡, ቃየል, ዘከመ፡ ቀተሎ፡ ቃየል፡ ለአቤል።, አቤል፡, ቃየል, and ቋዕ፡. ዘከመ፡ ተወክፈ፡ ቍርባነ፡ አቤል።
God instructs Enoch on how to invoke his name as Seth looks at the recumbent body of his dead brother Abel. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ሄኖክ።, ሴት፡, አቤል፡, and ዘከመ፡ ተወልደ፡ ሴት፡ እምድኅረ፡ ሞተ፡ አቤል።

Jared and his children are shown above, while below the children of Seth descend from the Mount of the Treasures to meet the wives of Cain, one of whom is a giant. Captions: ያሬድ።, እግዚአብሔር።, and ዘከመ፡ ወረዱ፡ ደቂቀ፡ ሴት፡ እምደብር።. ዘከመ፡ ዓቀቦ፡ ለያሬድ፡ በደብረ፡ መዛግብት።
God, who sits on a cloak that symbolizes his blessing, addresses Enoch in a garden with trees and flowers. Captions: ሄኖክ፡, እግዚአብሔር።, and መልአክ።. ዘከመ፡ አፍለሶ፡ ለሄኖክ፡ ውስተ፡ ብሔረ፡ ሕያዋን።

God, who holds a gold orb, grants a long life to Methuselah who prays towards him with a group of elders, presumably his children. Captions: ማቱሳላ፡, መላእክት፤, አበው፡, and እግዚአብሔር፡. ዘከመ፡ ወሀቦ፡ ኑኃ፡ መዋዕል፡ ለማቱሳላ።

The Ark of Noah described as a tābot and filled with humans, cattle, horses, domesticated animals, and wild beasts. A second group of people are drowning. Captions: ኖኅ።, እግዚአብሔር፡, ታቦት፤, ሰብእ።, እንስሳ። አራዊት፤, and ማየ፡ አይኅ።. ዘከመ፡ አኀብአ፡ ለኖኅ፡ ውስተ፡ ከርሠ፡ ታቦት።
God decides to dwell in the house of Shem, which is represented like a tent, rather than in that of Japhet or Ham. Captions: ካም፡, ያፌት፤, ብእሲቱ ሴም፡, እግዚአብሔር።, and ሐይመት፡. ዘከመ፡ ኃደረ፡ ውስተ፡ ሐይመቱ፡ ለሴም።
God entrusts Adam’s tomb to Melchizedek and appoints him as a priest. Captions: መልከ፡ ጼዴቅ።, እግዚአብሔር።, and መቃብረ፡ አዳም።. ዘከመ፡ ዓቀበ፡ መልከ፡ ጼዴቅ፡ መቃብረ፡ አዳም።
God readies himself to scatter the people who are building the Tower of Babel. Captions: ጥቅመ፡ ስናዖር። and እግዚአብሔር።. ዘከመ፡ ዘረወ፡ *ልሳነ፡ ኵሉ፡ አንጋድ።

God calls , who is accompanied by his wife , to the land of , which is populated with trees and located between two rivers. Captions: ሳራ፡, አብርሃም፡, እግዚአብሔር።, and ፪ቱ፡ አፍላግ።. ዘከመ፡ አውፅኦ፡ ለአብርሃም፡ እምከላውዴዎን።

God, placed between the two columns of text, looks at the five cities of the plain engulfed by fires (above); Lot and his daughters and wife, who turns back, are guided away by the two angels (below). Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ሎጥ፡, ዘከመ፡ ኮነት፡ ብእሲቱ፡ ሐውልተ፡ ፄው፡ ሶበ፡ ተመይጠት፡ ድኅሬሃ፡, ሎጥ፡, ፪ኤ፡ አዋልዲሁ፡, and ዘከመ፡ መርህዎ፡ መላእክት።. ዘከመ፡ አዝነመ፡ እሳተ፡ ዲበ፡ ሰዶም፡ ወገሞራ።
Two scenes: gives one of her slaves to (left); the pair look at the newly born Isaac (right). Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ሳራ፡, አብርሃም፡, ሳራ፡, ይስሐቅ።, አብርሃም፡, and ንብቲራ፡. ዘከመ፡ ወሀባ፡ ወልደ፡ ለሳራ፡ ድኅረ፡ ኃለፈ፡ መዋዕሊሃ።
The Binding of Isaac. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።m አብርሃም፡, ይስሐቅ።, በግዕ፡, ዕፀ፡ ሳቤቅ፡, አብርሃም፡, and ይስሐቅ።. ዘከመ፡ ቤዘዎ፡ ለይስሐቅ፡ በመሥዋዕተ፡ በግዕ።

Through the intervention of Michael the Archangel neither Esau nor Laban harm Jacob, who is depicted in front of a tent, together with his two wives and his large flock of animals. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል።, ኤሳው።, ላባ፡, ሐይመት።, ራሔል፡, ያዕቆብ፡, ለያ፡, አፍራስ፡, አግማል፡, አባግዕ፡, and ዕድግት፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖ፡ ለያዕቆብ፡ እምእደ፡ ኤሳው፡ እኁሁ።
The devil tests Job. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ሰይጣን፡ (twice), ብእሲቱ፡ ለኢዮብ፤, ሕማሙ፡ ለኢዮብ፡, and ሳፍር፡ ኤልፋዝ፡ በልዳዶስ።. ዘከመ፡ ሐመ፡ ኢዮብ።

Three episodes: Joseph is sold by his brothers (above); his brothers depart with grain in their sacks as well as the objects placed there by his men and are chased by one of his stewards (bottom left); Joseph confronts his brothers. Captions: ዘከመ፡ ሤጥዎ፡ ለዮሴፍ፡ አኃዊሁ፡, እግዚአብሔር።, አግማል፡, ዕድግት፡, and ዮሴፍ፡. ዘከመ፡ አንገሦ፡ ለዮሴፍ፡ በብሔረ፡ ግብጽ።

To the left God encounters Moses on a mountain with his flock of sheep. To the right the latter sets off towards Egypt. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፤, ሙሴ፡ (twice), and አባግዕ።. ዘከመ፡ ፈነዎ፡ ለሙሴ፡ እምብሔረ፡ ምድያም። ውስተ፡ ምድረ፡ ግብጽ።
God observers the suffering of the Israelites and listens to their pleas. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡ and እስራኤል፤. ዘከመ፡ ወረደ፡ ውስተ፡ ምድረ፡ ግብጽ፡ ከመ፡ ያድኅኖሙ፡ ለሕዝቡ።

Moses confronts the Pharaoh and his officials. His staff has become a snake that swallows the snake conjured from a staff by two magicians. In the bottom right corner, the livestock of the Egyptians are dead because of the fifth plague. Captions: መልአክ፤, ፈርዖን, እግዚአብሔር፡, ሙሴ፡, ግብጻውያን።, and ከይሲ፡. ዘከመ፡ ቀሠፎሙ፡ ለግብጻውያን።
The Crossing of the Red Sea. Captions: ዘከመ፡ አስጠሞ፡ ለፈርዖን፡ ምስለ፡ ሠራዊቱ።, ፈርዖን, እግዚአብሔር።, እስራኤል፡, and ኤርትራ፡. ዘከመ፡ ከፈለ፡ ባሕረ፡ ኤርትራ።

The people of Israel celebrate the Pharaoh’s defeat by singing while Miriam beats a drum before her brothers. Captions: ሙሴ፡, ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, እስራኤል።, ከበሮ፡, and እኅተ፡ ሙሴ።. ዘከመ፡ ሰብሕዎ፡ እስራኤል፡ በጽንፈ፡ ባሕር።
God creates clouds to shield the Israelites from the heat of the sun. Captions: ደመና፡, እግዚአብሔር።, እስራኤል፡, ሙሴ፡, and ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል።. ዘከመ፡ ጸለሎሙ፡ ደመና፡ ለእስራኤል።
The Israelites eat manna and drink the water that miraculously pours out from two mountains. Captions: ሙሴ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ኰኵሐ፡, ሙሴ፡, ማይ፡, and እስራኤል፡. ዘከመ፡ አውኀዘ፡ ማየ፡ እምኰኵሕ።

A priest holding a torch and Aaron holding the budded staff and a censer stand before God while Korah and his followers burn in an underground fire. Captions: አሮን፡, እግዚአብሔር።, and በከመ፡ አውዓዮ፡ ለቆራ፡ ምስለ፡ ሕዝቡ፡. ዘከመ፡ ቀደሰ፡ ክህነቶ፡ ለአሮን።
An unidentified individual, probably Joshua, guides the leaders of the Israelites across the Promised Land. Captions: እግዚአብሔር። and እስራኤል፡. ዘከመ፡ ወሀቦሙ፡ ለሕዝቡ፡ አህጉረ፡ ፴ ነገሥት።
The crossing of the Jordan River. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, እስራኤል፡, ዮርዳኖስ።, and ኤያሱ።. ዘከመ፡ ዓቀመ፡ ተከዜ፡ ዮርዳኖስ፤
The fall of Jericho. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, እስራኤል፡, ሰብአ፡ ኤሪኮ፡, and ‘Fall of Jericho’ (the latter in English). በከመ፡ አንሐለ፡ ጥቅመ፡ ኢያሪኮ።
The prostitute Rahab looks at the Israelites killing the men and rulers of Jericho. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ራአብ፡ ዘማ፡, እስራኤል፡, ሰብአ፡ ኢያሪኮ፡. በከመ፡ አድኃና፡ ለረአብ፡ ዘማ፡ አመ፡ ተሐጕሉ፡ ዓላውያን።
Two scenes: Joshua makes peace with the people of Gibeon (above); and the sun stands still for him and his army (below). Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, በከመ፡ ቆመ፡ ፀሐይ፡ ለኢያሱ።, and እስራኤል፡. ዘከመ፡ አብኦሙ፡ ለኢያሱ፡ ወለካሌብ።

Two scenes: Balaam and the angel (left); and Phinehas raises his spear to kill an Israelite man who is copulating with a Midianite woman before a golden idol, while another one performs a sacrifice below (right). Captions: እስራኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, በለዓም፡, ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል።, ዕድግት፡, ፊንሐስ፡ ዘምሪ፡, ከስቢ፡, ጣዖት፡, and መሥዋዕተ፡ ጣዖት፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖሙ፡ ለእስራኤል፡ በእደ፡ ፊንሐስ፡ እምሕማመ፡ ብድብድ።
Jael drives a peg through the head of Sisera. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, እስራኤል፡, ዲቦራ፡, ሐይመት፡, ሕዝበ፡ ሲሳራ፡, ኢያዔል፡, and ሲሳራ፡. ዘከመ፡ ቀተለቶ፡ ኢያኤል፡ ለሲሳራ፡

Three scenes showing military leaders: Jephthah sacrifices his daughter who had come out to meet him (upper left); Gideon defeats the Midianites (bottom left); and Samson defeats the Philistines brandishing the donkey jaw. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ዮፍታሔ፡, መንከሰ፡ ዓድግ፡, ጌዴዎን፡, ሰብአ፡ ጌዴዎን፡, ሰብአ፡ ምድያም፡, ሶምሶን፡, and ሰብአ፡ ኢሎፍሊ፡. ዘከመ፡ ዓቀቦሙ፡ እምፀር፡ ለእስራኤል፡ በእደ፡ ጌዴዎን፡ ወዮፍታሔ፤ ወሶምሶን።

Four scenes: Samuel addresses the Israelites after the death of Eli (upper left); the five Philistine kings send the Ark of the Covenant back to Israel (bottom left); king Saul addresses an armed man, probably his son (upper right); and the Israelites defeat the Philistines (bottom right). Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ሳሙኤል፡, እስራኤል፡, ኤሊ፡, ሳኦል፡, ታቦት፡, አፍኒን፡ ፊንሐስ፡, and ሰብአ፡ ኢሎፍሊ፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖሙ፡ ለእስራኤል፡ በእደ፡ ሳሙኤል፡ እምኢሎፍሊ።

Three scenes: David plays for King Saul who hurls a spear at him (upper left); David defeats Goliath who is wearing a chainmail and pointed helmet and the Philistines flee (bottom); Joab hurls his spear at Absalom who is hanging from a tree. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, በከመ፡ አድኃኖ፡ ለዳዊት፡ እምእደ፡ ሳኦል፡, እስራኤል፡, ዳዊት፡, ኢሎፍሊ፡, ጎልያድ፡, ኢዮአብ፡, and አቤሴሎም፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖ፡ ለዳዊት፡ እምእደ፡ ጎልያድ፡ ወእምአቤሴሎም፡ ወልዱ፤

Five scenes: David interacts with two unidentified men (his courtiers or soldiers); Uriah dies in battle; David marries Batsheba; Nathan reproaches the king for his behaviour; and David lies on the ground to seek forgiveness. Captions: ዳዊት፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ኦርዮ፡, ቤርሳቤሕ፡, ዳዊት፡, ዳዊት፡, ናታን፡, and ዘከመ፡ ወድቀ፡ ዳዊት፡. ዘከመ፡ ሰረየ፡ ኃጢአተ፡ ዳዊት፡ ንጉሥ።
Solomon is appointed king over the kings of the world. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ሰሎሞን፡, and ነገሥተ፡ ምድር፡. በከመ፡ ረሰዮ፡ መክብበ፡ ነገሥት፡ ለሰሎሞን።
The Kings of Israel pray to God. Captions: እግዚአብሔር። and ነገሥታት።. ዘከመ፡ ሰምዓ፡ ጸሎቶሙ፡ ለነገሥተ፡ እስራኤል፡ በበጊዜሁ።
The Prophets of Israel have visions with the just in Paradise and the sinners in Hell. Captions: ነቢያት፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ጻድቃን፤, ኃጥአን፡, and ገሃነመ፡ እሳት፡. ዘከመ፡ ኃረዮሙ፡ ለነቢያት፡ ከመ፡ ይርአዩ፡ ዕሤተ፡ ጻድቃን፡ ወፍዳ፡ ለኃጥአን፤

Three scenes: King Ahab asks for Naboth’s vineyard; the latter is killed; and dogs devour the decapitated body of the king’s wife Jezebel. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, አክዓብ፡, ኤልዛቤል፡ (twice), and ናቡቴ፡. ዘከመ፡ ተበቀለ፡ ቤተ፡ አክዓብ፡ በእንተ፡ ግፍዓ፡ ናቡቴ።

Three scenes: God instructs Elijah ; the prophet confronts King Ahab; the king, who has now aged, sits afraid outside the city walls. Captions: እግዚአብሔር።, ኤልያስ፡, አክዓብ፡ ኤልያስ፡, and ዘከመ፡ ደንገፀ፡ ከዓብ።. ዘከመ፡ ነገሮ፡ ለኤልያስ፡ ከመ፡ ደንገፀ፡ አክዓብ።

Three scenes: Elijah in the wilderness speaks to God and is aided by an angel; Elijah appoints Elisha as his disciple; the Aramean soldiers are unable to seize Elisha. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, መልአክ፡, and ኤ. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖ፡ ለኤልያስ፡ እምእደ፡ አ

Three scenes: Jonah and the big fish; God instructs Jonah; and a naked Jonah confronts the Ninevites before the leafy plant. Captions: ሐመር፡, ዮናስ፡, አንበሪ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ሰብአ፡ ነነዌ፡, and እሳት፡. ዘከመ፡ አውፅኦ፡ እምከርሠ፡ አንበሪ፡ ለዮናስ።

God addresses the Two Tribes of Israel that are left after the other Ten Tribes are deported by the Assyrian King Shalmaneser. Captions: ፪ አንጋደ፡ እስራኤል።, እግዚአብሔር፡, ስልምናሶር፡, and ፄዋ፡ እስራኤል።. ዘከመ፡ ባልሖሙ፡ ለ፪ አንጋድ። አመ፡ ፄወዎሙ፡ ስልምናሶር፡ ለ፲ አንጋደ፡ እስራኤል፤

King Hezekiah prays to God who sends an angel to kill the men of the Assyrian ruler Sennacherib in their encampment who is later beheaded by two of his sons while praying to an idol. Captions: ዘከመ፡ ጸለየ፡ ሕዝቅያስ፡ ወኢሳይያስ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, መልአክ፡, ዘከመ፡ ቀተልዎ፡ ደቂቁ፡ ለሰናክሬም፡ ውስተ፡ ቤተ፡ ጣዖት፡, and ሠራዊተ፡ ሰናክሬም. ዘከመ፡ ቀተለ፡ ትዕይንተ፡ ሰናክሬም፡ በሰይፈ፡ መልአክ። ሶበ፡ ርእየ፡ ት፡ዕይርቶ።
Manasseh prays to God and is restored to his kingdom. Captions: ዘከመ፡ ጸለየ፡ ምናሴ።, እግዚአብሔር፡, ዘከመ፡ ተሞቅሐ፡, and ዘከመ፡ ተመይጠ፡ ኀበ፡ መንግሥቱ።. ዘከመ፡ ፈትሖ፡ ለምናሴ፡ እሙቃሔሁ።
An angel sent by God cleanses Isaiah of leprosy by touching his mouth with a hot piece of coal held with thongs. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ጒጠት፡, ኢሳይያስ፡, and መልአክ፡. ዘከመ፡ አንጽሖ፡ ለኢሳይያስ፡ እምለምጹ።
Josiah celebrates the Passover by having lambs and cattle sacrificed and by burning offerings to God. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ኢዮስያስ።, እሳት፡, and መሥዋዕተ፡ አባግዕ፡ ወአልህምት።. ዘከመ፡ ተወክፈ፡ መሥዋዕተ፡ ኢዮስያስ።

God saves Jeremiah from the mud cistern in which he was thrown by officials of King Zedekiah. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል፡, ሴዴቅያስ።, ዘከመ፡ ወፅአ፡ እምዓዘቅት፡, and ዘከመ፡ ተወድየ፡ ውስተ፡ ዓዘቅት።. ዘከመ፡ አውፅኦ፡ ለኤርምያስ፡ እምግበ፡ ዓምዓም፡ ሶበ፡ ወደዮ፡ ሴዴቅያስ፤

Three scenes: the miraculous sleep of Ebed-Melech (Abimelech) the Ethiopian; God speaks to Baruch through Jeremiah; and the Israelites, along with their blind and shackled king, are taken captive by King Nebuchadnezzar who rides among his armed men who are shown holding guns, shields, and spears. Captions: አቤሜሌክ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, መላእክት፡, ባሮክ፡, ኤርምያስ፡, እስራኤል፡, ናቡከደነ፡ ጻር፡, and ዘከመ፡ ተዓወረ፡ ሴዴቅያስ።. ዘከመ፡ ተፄወዉ፡ እስራኤል፡ በእደ፡ ናቡከደነ፡ ጾር።
A miniature showing the Three Youths in the fiery Furnace. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል፡, ናቡከደነ፡ ጻር፡, ፫ ደቂቅ፡, እቶነ፡ እሳት፡, and ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖሙ፡ ለ፫ ደቂቅ፡ እምእቶነ፡ እሳት።
God transforms King Nebuchadnezzar into a wild boar. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ናቡከደነ፡ ጻር፡, መልአክ።, and ዘከመ፡ ሐራውያ፡. ዘከመ፡ ኮነ፡ ናቡከደነ፡ ጾር፡ አርዌ፡ ምድር።

Two scenes: God turns King Nebuchadnezzar back into a human being; and the king is crowned and enthroned and attended by several courtiers including Daniel and the Three Youths. Captions: ናቡከደነ፡ ጻር፡ ዘይቤ፡ መንግሥቱ፡ ዘለዓለም። ወምኵናኑኒ፡ ለትውልደ፡ ትውልድ።, እግዚአብሔር፡, ዳንኤል፡, ናቡከደነጻር፡, and ፫ ደቂቅ፡. ዘከመ፡ ሜጦ፡ ለናቡከደነ፡ ጾር፡ እምግዕዘ፡ አርዌ፡ ኀበ፡ ዘቀዳሚ፡ ግዕዙ።

Two scenes: Daniel listens to the accounts of Susanna and the two elders; and the two elders are stoned to death for their lies. Captions: ዳንኤል፡, ረበናት፡, ሶስና፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, and ዘከመ፡ ወገርዎሙ፡ ለረበናት፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃና፡ ለሶስና፡ እምእደ፡ ረበናት።
Daniel in the lion’s den. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል፡, ዘከመ፡ ወደይዎ፡ ለዳንኤል፡ ውስተ፡ ግበ፡ አናብስት።, መልአክ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል።, ግብ፡, and አናብስት፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖ፡ ለዳንኤል፡ እምአፈ፡ አናብስት፡ ርኁባን፡ ሐራ፡ ዳርዮስ፡ አመ፡ ወደይዎ።
Habakkuk brings bread in a basket to Daniel. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል፡, መልአክ፡, ዕንባቆም፡, ዘከመ፡ ቦአ፡ ዳንኤል፡ ወስተ፡ ግበ፡ አናብስት፡, and መሶብ፡ ወርቅ. ዘከመ፡ አጾሮ፡ ለዕንባቆም፡ ነቢይ፡ ምሳሐ፡ ዳንኤል።

Two scenes: Ezekiel has a vision of God surrounded by the tetramorph; and Ezekiel dreams of the Valley of Dry Bones. Captions: ገጸ፡ ሰብእ።, ገጸ፡ ንስር፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ሕዝቅኤል፡, ገጸ፡ አንበሳ፡, ገጸ፡ ላህም፡, ሕዝቅኤል፡, and ምውታን፡. ዘከመ፡ አንሥአ፡ በእደ፡ ሕዝቅኤል፡ አዕፅምተ፡ ምውታን።

Two scenes: a group of three prophets, including Enoch, Elijah, and Ezra, converses before God; Ezra writes his book, an additional empty sheet of parchment at his side is represented as an empty rectangle. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል፡, ሄኖክ፡, ኤልያስ፡, እዝራ፡, and ዘከመ፡ ጸሐፈ፡ እዝራ፡ መጽሕፍተ፡ ነቢያት። እምድኅረ፡ አውዓይዎሙ በእሳት።. ዘከመ፡ አልዓሎ፡ ለእዝራ፡ ኀበ፡ ሀለዉ፡ ሄኖክ፡ ወኤልያስ፤

Jeconiah is freed from prison during the reign of Amel-Marduk and after the death of the latter’s father, Nebuchadnezzar. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ሰብአ፡ ባቢሎን፡, ኢኮንያን፡, ኤዎልማሮዴቅ፡, and ዘከመ፡ ሞተ፡ ከደነ፡ ጾር፡. ዘከመ፡ ፈትሖ፡ ለኢኮንያን፡ እማእሰሩ።

Five scenes: the hand of the angel of god writes a prophecy about the imminent death of Belshazzar during the banquet at the later’s court; Belshazzar’s dignitaries and wives participate in the banquet; the cup of God is brought to the banquet; Belshazzar’s beheaded body; Belshazzar’s head is shown to the people of Israel. Captions: መልአክ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ዘከመ፡ ጸሐፈ፡ እደ፡ መልአክ፡ ወይቤ፡ ማኔ፡ ቴቄል፡ ፋሬስ።, ብላጣሶር።, ብእሲቱ፡ ዘከመ፡ አኀዘተ፡ ጽዋዓ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡, ዘከመ፡ ወሀቦሙ፡ ርእሶ፡ ለእስራኤል፡ ወዓለሁ፡, ዘከመ፡ አምስሐ፡ መኳንንቲሁ፡, ዘከመ፡ መጠዋ፡ ጽዋዓ፡, and ዘከመ፡ ፈከረ፡ ሎቱ፡ ዳንኤል፡ ሕልማ፡. ዘከመ፡ መተረ፡ ርእሰ፡ ብልጣሶር፡ በሰይፈ፡ ርእሱ።

King Cyrus announces to the Israelites that they may return home; one of his officers plays a drum. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, እስራኤል፡, ቂሮስ, እስራኤል፡, and ዘከመ፡ ዜነወ፡ ቃለ፡ ዓዋዲ፡ ሚጠቶሙ፡ ለእስራኤል፡. ዘከመ፡ አስተጋብኦሙ፡ ለእስራኤል፡ ድኅረ፡ ፄዋዌ።
Zorobabel leads the Jews back from captivity. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ትሩፋነ፡ ባቢሎን፡, and ዮሴዕ፡ ወልደ፡ ዮሴዴቅ፡. ዘከመ፡ ሜጦሙ፡ ለእስራኤል፡ እምፄዋዌ።

Two scenes: the Israelites return to Jerusalem led by Joshua, Zorobabel, and a third unidentified leader; and a battle between the Israelites and the people of Gog. Captions: ዮሴዕ፡, ዘሩባቤል፡, and ሰብአ፡ ጎግ።. ዘከመ፡ ወሀቦሙ፡ ምህርካ፡ ነኪራን፡ ሰብአ፡ ጎግ፡ ለዘሩባቤል፡ ወዮሴዕ።

Three scenes: Judith meets Holofernes in his camp; she beheads him; and her slave brings his head back to the town in the food bag. Captions: ዮዲት፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ሆሎፎርኒስ፡, ዘከመ፡ ቀተለቶ፡ ዮዲት፡ ለሆሎፎርኒስ፡, and ሐይመት፡. ዘከመ፡ ቀተሎ፡ በእደ፡ ዮዲት፡ ለሆሎፎርኒስ፡

Three scenes: an unidentified man, possibly Haman, and two attendants, among whom possibly Mordecai refusing to bow before him; Mordecai tells Queen Esther about a plot to kill the Israelites; Haman is hung on a forked pole; and King Ahasuerus praises Mordecai. Captions: አስቴር።, እግዚአብሔር፡, መርዶክዮስ፡, እስራኤል።, ዘከመ፡ ተሰቅለ፡ ሐማ፡, አርጤከስስ፡, and መርዶክዮስ፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖሙ፡ ለእስራኤል፡ ወለመርዶክዮስ፡ በቀቲለ፡ ፀሮሙ፡ ሐማ።

The Israelites rebuild the walls of Jerusalem in spite of the opposition of Sanballat and his soldiers. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ሰንበላጥ፡, እስራኤል፡, and ሠራዊተ፡ ሰንበላጥ፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖሙ፡ እምእደ፡ ሰንበላጥ፡ ለእለ፡ ወጠኑ፡ ሐኒፀ፡ ማኅደር፡ ካልእ፤

Two scenes: Alexander the Great has a vision of a sword-bearing angel; and he pays tribute with a gift to the high priest and his entourage. Captions: መልአክ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, እስክንድር፡, ሠራዊተ፡ , ዘከመ፡ ሰገደ፡ እስክንድር፡ ለሊቀ፡ ካህና ምስለ፡ አምኃ፡, and ካህናት፡. ዘከመ፡ አደንገፆ፡ ለእስክንድር፡ ንጉሥ፡ በርእየተ፡ ሰይፉ፡ ለመልአክ፤

Two scenes: Ptolemy II is inspired by God to commission the translation of the Bible into Greek; an angel stops Simeon from changing the text of Isaiah that claims a Virgin will bear a son. Captions: በጥሊሞስ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, መልአክ፡, ካልአኒሁ፡ ጸሐፍት፡, ስምዖን፡, and ሐይመት፡. ዘከመ፡ ወሀቦ፡ ለስምዖን፡ ብዙኃ፡ ዘመነ፡ እስከ፡ ትወልድ፡ ድንግል።

Antiochus IV puts the Israelites to death and has a wild board sacrificed in the Temple to desecrate it. Captions: ሥዕለ፡ ፀሐይ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, አንጥያኮስ፡, ዘከመ፡ አዘዞሙ፡ ይሡዑ፡ መሥዋዕተ፡ ጣዖት፡, and እስራኤል፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖሙ፡ ለመቃብያን፡ እምእደ፡ አንጥያኮስ።
Antiochus IV puts Lazarus and his children to death. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ደቂቀ፡ አልዓዛር፡, አልዓዛር፡, and መስብር፡. ዘከመ፡ ተወክፉ፡ ሥቃያተ፡ አልዓዛር፡ ወደቂቁ፡ ወብእሲቱ፡ በመንግሥቱ፡ ለአንጥያኮስ።
An angel brings heavenly bread to Mary in a basket. Captions: ቤተ፡ መቅደስ።, እግዚአብሔር፡, and ዘከመ፡ ወሀባ፡ መልአክ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ኅብስተ፡ ሰማያዌ።. ዘከመ፡ ኃረያ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ እምአንስተ፡ ዓለም።
The Annunciation of Gabrielto Mary who is reading the Book of Isaiah. Captions: እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም።, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል።, and መጽሐፈ፡ ኢሳይያስ፡. ዘከመ፡ ፈነወ፡ ለወልዱ፡ ከመ፡ ይልበስ፡ ሥጋ።

A Nativity scene with shepherds playing the Ethiopian game of gannā and a donor in proskynesis. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ዘከመ፡ ተወልደ፡ መድኃኒነ፡ ውስተ፡ ጎል፡, እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም።, በከመ፡ ተማኅፀነት፡ ወለተ፡ ጽዮን፡, ዮሴፍ፡, ላህም፡, ሰሎሜ፡, መድኃኒነ።, ዕድግት፡, and ኖሎት፡. ዘከመ፡ አርአዮሙ፡ ለኖሎት፡ ልደተ፡ ዚአሁ፡ በቤተ፡ ልሔም።
The circumcision of Jesus. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል።, ሰሎሜ።, እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም።, and መድኃኒነ፡. ዘከመ፡ ተገዝረ፡ እግዚእነ፡
The adoration of the Magi. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡, and ዘከመ፡ ወሀብዎ፡ ለእግዚእነ፡ አምኃ፡ ነገሥተ፡ ፋርስ። እንዘ፡ ይሰግዱ፡ ሎቱ።. ዘከመ፡ መርሖሙ፡ ለሰብአ፡ ሰገል፡ በትእምርተ፡ ኮከብ።
The Flight into Egypt. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ዘከመ፡ ዜነዎ፡ መልአክ፡ በሕልም፡ ለዮሴፍ፡, ሰሎሜ፡, ዮሴፍ፡, and እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ምስለ፡ ፍቁር፡ ወልዳ።. ዘከመ፡ አዘዘ፡ ለዮሴፍ፡ በአፈ፡ መልአክ፡ ከመ፡ ይንሣእ፡ ሕፃነ፡ ወእሞ፡ ወይጕየይ፡ ብሔረ፡ ግብጽ።

Herod commands the Massacre of the Innocents and the beheading of Zechariah in the Temple, while Elizabeth is able to escape. Captions: ሄሮድስ፡, ዘከመ፡ ቀተልዎ፡ ለዘካርያስ፡ በቤተ፡ መቅደስ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ኤልሳቤጥ፡, and ዘከመ፡ ተቀትሉ፡ ሕፃናት።. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖ፡ ለዮሐንስ፡ እምእደ፡ ሄሮድስ።
The Baptism of Jesus in the Jordan. Captions: ዘከመ፡ ወረደ፡ መንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ በአምሳለ፡ ርግብ፡, ዘከመ እግዚእነ፡, ዮሐንስ፡ መጥምቅ፡, and ዮርዳኖስ፡. ዘከመ፡ ተጠምቀ፡ እግዚእነ፡ በእደ፡ ዮሐንስ፡ መጥምቅ።
The Temptation of Jesus. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ሰይጣን፡, እግዚእነ፡, and ሰይጣን።. ዘከመ፡ ሞኦ፡ ለሰይጣን፡ በገዳም።
The Last Supper. Captions: እግዚእነ፡ and ሐዋርያት፡. በከመ፡ አርአዮሙ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ለአርዳኢሁ፡ ሥርዓተ፡ ቍርባን።
The Agony in the Garden. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, መልአክ፡ ዘወሀቦ፡ መስቀለ፡ ምስለ፡ ጽዋዕ።, እግዚእነ, ">ዮሐንስ፡ ፍቁረ፡ እግዚእነ፡, ያዕቆብ፡ ወልደ፡ ዘብዴዎስ።, and ቅዱስ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡. ዘከመ፡ ፈነወ፡ ኀቤሁ፡ መልአከ፡ ዘይቤሎ፡ ለከ፡ ኃያል፡ እግዚኦ።
A combination of the Anastasis and Resurrection of Jesus. Captions: መላእክት፡, እግዚእነ፡, መላእክት፡, and ዓቀብተ፡ መቃብር።. ዘከመ፡ ተንሥአ፡ እምውታን።
The Ascension of Jesus. Captions: እግዚእነ፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡, and ሐዋርያት። እለ፡ ነጸሩ፡ ዕርገተ፡ እግዚእነ፡. ዘከመ፡ ዓርገ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ውስተ፡ ሰማያት።
Pentecost. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ርደተ፡ ጰራቅሊጦስ፡ በአምሳለ፡ እሳት፡ ላዕለ፡ ሐዋርያት፡, and እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡. ዘከመ፡ ፈነወ፡ መንፈሶ፡ ቅዱሰ፡ ኀበ፡ አርዳኢሁ፡ በአምሳለ፡ እሳት።
The Second Coming of Jesus. Captions: ጻድቃን፡, ትእምርተ፡ ኵናት፡, እግዚእነ፡, ትእምርተ፡ መስቀል፡, ጻድቃን፡, and ኃጥአን።. ዘከመ፡ ይመጽእ፡ እግዚእነ፡ እንዘ፡ ያርኢ፡ ትእምርተ፡ ርግዘቱ፡ ወስቅለቱ።

The Last Judgment. In this miniature Christ is identified with the caption እግዚአብሔር፡ that is elsewhere reserved for the Father). Other captions: መላእክት፡ (twice), ጻድቃን፡, ኃጥአን፡, and ገሃነመ፡ እሳት፡. ዘከመ፡ ይመጽእ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ለኰንኖ።

The Trinity and a donor in proskynesis. Captions: ሥላሴ፡ ቅዱስ።, ገጸ፡ ሰብእ፡, ገጸ፡ ንስር፡, ገጸ፡ አንበሳ፡, ገጸ፡ ላህም፡, and በ ተክለ፡ ማርያም፡ . The last caption has been partially erased. ዘከመ፡ አስተርአዩ፡ ዲበ፡ መንበር፡ ልዑል፡ ሥሉስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ አኀዝያነ፡ ፍጥረት፤
The Martyrdom of Peter and Paul. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ዘከመ፡ ተሰቅለ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ቊልቊሊተ፡, መልአክ፡, መልአክ፡, and ዘከመ፡ ተመትረ፡ በሰይፍ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ጳውሎስ።. ዘከመ፡ አጽንዖሙ፡ በገድል፡ ለጴጥሮስ፡ ወለጳውሎስ።

A group of unidentified martyrs and saints with Gabra Manfas Qǝddus and St George. Captions: ጻድቃን፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, አቡነ፡ ገብረ፡ መንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡, ሰማዕታት፡, and ቅዱስ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡. ዘከመ፡ ኃደጎሙ፡ ለጻድቃን፡ ወሰማዕት፡ አምሳለ፡ ብሩር፡ ይትፈተኑ፡ በእሳት።
God saves a group of martyrs from lions and flames. Captions: ቅዱስ፡ ሚካኤል፡, እግዚአብሔር፡, ቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል።, አናብስት፡, ሰማዕት፡ ወጻድቃን።, and እሳት፡. ዘከመ፡ አድኃኖሙ፡ እምአፈ፡ አንበሳ፡ ወእሳት፡ ለፍቁራኒሁ፡ ጻድቃን፡ ወሰማዕት።

God the Father flanked by ʾAbuna Zamalakot and enthroned above the donor, Takla Māryām, in proskynesis. Captions: እግዚአብሔር፡, ገጸ፡ ሰብእ፡, ገጸ፡ ንስር፡, ገጸ፡ አንበሳ፡, ገጸ፡ ላህም፡, በከመ፡ ከሠተ፡ ሎቱ፡ ዘንተ፡ ድርሳነ፡ ለአቡነ፡ ዘመለኮት።, and በከመ፡ ተክለ፡ ማርያም፡ . The last caption has been partially erased. ዘከመ፡ አስተርአየ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ በዋሕድናሁ።

item.desc: Ownership note written in brownish ink in the upper margin, stating that the manuscript belonged to .

The incipits of stanzas 8 and 9 of the Lamentation of the Soul (Saqoqāwa nafs) are indicated with Ethiopic numbers on the left margin of . Rubrication has not been executed on , leading to textual lacunas. Cues for the rubricator are found on the left margin of . Corrections are scarce. They mostly consist of interlinear additions (e.g. ), erasures (e.g. ), or are made by encircling the word (). Pen trial in red and black ink on .

The name of the original owner or patron, , is inserted in the main hand on as well as in the supplication formula on the upper margin of , here indicated as ገብርከ፡, ‘your servant’. The name of a later owner, , is indicated on on a previously erased name.

Several white stickers are glued on the inner side of the front cover. On the rightmost is printed ‘H. Kevorkian collection’, with the number ‘623’ added in pencil. On the following sticker are written in blue pen the numbers ‘1977’ and ‘18’, above a previous signature ‘MS Et 16’ in black ink. On the leftmost is printed the name of (ቤንት፡ ዩውል፡ የንሰን።), with the signature ‘MS. Aeth. d. 16’ added in pencil. Below, a further sticker is glued with the shelfmark ‘Ms 36’ printed on it. The inner side of the front cover and of the front board also bear three barely legible black stamps.
Two white stickers are glued on the inner side of the back board. On the right one is written ‘489’ in black pen, on the left one is printed the number ‘1067’.

binding.decoNote: The book is bound in wooden boards, fully covered with reddish-brown blind-tooled leather.
The boards are slightly larger than the bookblock.

Full reddish-brown blind-tooled leather cover. The turn-ins have been trimmed out, and they form butt mitres at the corners. The external surfaces of the cover show a fine blind-tooled decoration. The decoration consists of seven concentric frames surrounding a central rectangular panel featuring a Latin cross. Frames composed by the repetition of single tools, enclosed between triple straight lines, alternate with empty ones. Proceeding from the outer to the inner frame, the repeating decoration motifs are criss-crosses, double X-forms, wavy lines, and curve strapwork elements. straight strapwork elements. The cross is formed by X-form motifs between triple straight lines. At the ends of the arms of the cross there are pairs of crescents disposed to resemble a corniform motif and three double circles. Triple straight lines divide the area around the cross in four panels, outlining the shape of the cross. Double circles are also stamped at the intersection of the triple straight lines. On the spine, triple straight lines intersect, forming a diamond pattern.
Chainstitch sewing on four sewing stations (two pairs); periodic fold pattern; Z-plied threads. Between each pair of sewing stations, there are two thread lengths.
The spine is formed by two overlapping layers of leather. The endbands are sewn through the first layer so that the tiedowns are hidden under the second layer.

history.origin: eighteenth century The manuscript can be dated palaeographically to the eighteenth century.
history.provenance: The manuscript was primarily owned or commissioned by a certain , whose name is inserted in the main hand on . It was then owned by a certain , whose name is written on on a previously erased name. The involvement of a certain ʾAbuna Zamalakot is evidenced by the mention of his name, written in the main hand in the miniature on . The latter is possibly identical to Mamhǝr Zamalakot, who was the abbot of the monastery of Dāgā ʾƎsṭifānos in the early eighteenth century (cp. 256, 307). This suggests that the manuscript has some connections to Dāgā ʾƎsṭifānos. At a certain time in its history, the manuscript came into the possession of the church of Maqdala Madḫāne ʿĀlam (see Additional note 1). The manuscript was then brought to England after the 1868 expedition by Napier. The subsequent owners are unknown in detail. At some time the book entered ’s private collection, as indicated on one of the white stickers glued on the inner side of the front cover (‘H. Kevorkian collection 623’). The book was sold to the and eventually purchased by at an auction at Sotheby’s on 20 March 1983, according to the auction catalogue.
history.acquisition: The manuscript entered the Bodleian Libraries as part of the bequest of after his death in 2006.
